sourceMovie.title: Molly's Game
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Molly's Game for the same reason it broke through in 2017: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a crime rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Aaron Sorkin keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The drama and crime blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Training Day
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Molly's Game clicked for you, Training Day (2001) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama and crime, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Molly's Game?
faq.answer: Try Training Day, Casino, Donnie Brasco—each shares DNA with Molly's Game while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Molly's Game?
faq.answer: Molly's Game (2017) is best described as Drama, Crime. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Molly's Game worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.2/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
